articles.title: US and Iran reach deal to end war; Trump declares it 'complete', orders Strait of Hormuz reopened and naval blockade lifted
articles.summary: The United States and Iran announced an agreement to end the war that began on February 28, with President Trump declaring 'The Deal with the Islamic Republic of Iran is now complete' and authorizing the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the immediate removal of the US naval blockade on Iranian ports. Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi confirmed the text of the memorandum of understanding was finalized. A formal signing ceremony is scheduled for Switzerland on Friday, June 19, with Pakistan and Qatar as lead mediators.

articles.title: US says Trump, Vance and Iran's Ghalibaf 'digitally' signed the Iran deal
articles.summary: US officials said President Trump, Vice President Vance and Iranian official Ghalibaf signed the agreement electronically the previous day, ahead of a formal in-person ceremony planned for Switzerland on Friday. The deal terms reportedly include opening the Strait of Hormuz, ending the US blockade of Iranian ports, and a 60-day ceasefire extension. Pakistan and Qatar are serving as witnesses and mediators.

articles.title: Pakistani PM announces US-Iran 'Peace Deal' includes a Lebanon truce
articles.summary: Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced the peace deal between the United States and Iran had been reached and said it provides for the 'immediate and permanent termination of military operations on all fronts, including in Lebanon.' A US official confirmed the deal 'includes Lebanon' but stressed Israel retains a right to self-defense. The war was launched on February 28 by the US and Israel against Iran.

articles.title: Israel says it won't be bound by Iran deal in Lebanon; Katz vows military will stay in the south
articles.summary: Defense Minister Israel Katz said Monday the Israeli military will remain in southern Lebanon 'despite all the existing pressures,' warning that if Iran strikes, Israel will hit it 'with full force.' Ministers said Israel will not consider itself bound by the US-Iran agreement's Lebanon provisions, even as the government instructed the IDF to avoid actions that could derail the emerging deal. Israel is seeking to separate the Lebanon file from the Iran talks.

articles.title: Israeli opposition calls US-Iran deal 'greatest strategic failure in Israel's history', demands Netanyahu's ouster
articles.summary: Opposition leaders and some coalition partners assailed Prime Minister Netanyahu after the US-Iran agreement, calling it the 'greatest strategic failure in Israel's history.' Yair Lapid said it was 'the greatest failure in foreign policy of any Israeli prime minister' and demanded Netanyahu's removal, accusing him of selling Washington an overly optimistic scenario and losing US trust mid-war. Democrats party leader Yair Golan said Israelis were 'waking up to an agreement made over Israel's head.'

articles.title: Oil prices plunge as US-Iran deal reopens the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oil prices fell sharply in early Monday Asian trading after the US and Iran announced a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz more than 100 days after its closure. Brent crude dropped about 3.95% to around $83.88 a barrel and WTI fell about 4.62% to roughly $80.96. Energy analysts cautioned it could take months before shipping and energy operations through the waterway fully normalize.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia welcomes US-Iran agreement, praises Trump and Pakistani mediation
articles.summary: Saudi Foreign Minister Faisal bin Farhan said the Kingdom 'highly appreciates' President Trump's decision to give diplomacy a chance to end the war and restore freedom of maritime navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. Riyadh also commended Pakistan's mediation and urged Iran to seize the opportunity to avoid further escalation and respond to negotiations for a comprehensive agreement.

articles.title: US-Iran ceasefire announcement raises hopes in Lebanon, but Israel says it won't withdraw
articles.summary: The US-Iran ceasefire announcement raised hopes in Lebanon, though analysts cautioned there is no binding deal until it is formally signed. Most nations welcomed the agreement as a step toward peace, but Israel said it will not withdraw from Lebanon. Israeli strikes continued in the Naqoura and Nabatieh districts of southern Lebanon, with at least one person killed.

articles.title: Israeli forces consolidate positions near Nabatieh as displaced residents trickle back
articles.summary: Israeli forces consolidated strategic positions around the Beaufort Ridge and Wadi Saluki north of the Litani River after issuing forced displacement orders for 29 locations in southern Lebanon. On Monday, Lebanese soldiers manned a checkpoint beside a Hezbollah flag at the entrance to Nabatieh as some displaced residents began returning to the battered southern city despite the continued Israeli presence.

articles.title: Palestinian death toll in Gaza tops 73,000 as Israeli strikes continue despite ceasefire
articles.summary: Gaza health officials said the Palestinian death toll has surpassed 73,000 as Israeli strikes continued despite the October 2025 ceasefire. Hamas disputes elements of the disarmament talks, arguing it should not implement the deal's second phase while Israel has yet to meet first-phase commitments including expanded humanitarian aid, troop withdrawals, reopening Gaza's main crossing, and a halt to strikes.

articles.title: Israel vows to keep occupying territory in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria regardless of Iran deal
articles.summary: Middle East Eye reported that Israeli officials say Israel will continue to hold territory it controls in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria even if the US-Iran deal is finalized. Israel is seeking to keep the Lebanon file separate from the Iran negotiations, while Hezbollah is pushing to fold it into a broader agreement. Israeli forces have entrenched military positions across the Gaza Strip and southern Lebanon since the October 2025 truce.

articles.title: Netherlands memorial commemorates Palestinian children and journalists killed in Israeli attacks
articles.summary: A memorial event in the central Markt square in Den Bosch, Netherlands, commemorated Palestinian children and journalists killed in Israeli attacks on Gaza. The gathering followed a weekend protest in Stockholm where hundreds rallied against Israeli operations in the Palestinian territories and Lebanon, carrying banners reading 'Children are being killed in Gaza' and 'Stop the attacks on Lebanon.'

articles.title: Gulf states look beyond the Strait of Hormuz as Iran emerges weakened from the war
articles.summary: Analysis pieces noted that with Iran weakened after the war, Gulf states are accelerating plans to diversify energy export routes that bypass the Strait of Hormuz, whose closure for more than 100 days sent fuel prices soaring and disrupted the global economy. The reopening agreed in the US-Iran deal is expected to ease prices, though full normalization of shipping is likely to take months.
